nodes.name: 脊索动物门
nodes.summary: 脊索动物门（Chordata）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联10767种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭等10767种鸟类。

nodes.name: 森林砍伐
nodes.summary: 原生林被砍伐导致栖息地面积和连通性下降。

nodes.name: 鸟纲
nodes.summary: 鸟纲（Aves）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联10767种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭等10767种鸟类。

nodes.name: 汶莱
nodes.summary: 汶莱是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联101种鸟类，包括婆羅洲地杜鵑、Short-toed coucal、Raffles's malkoha等101种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 东南亚
nodes.summary: 东南亚是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联160种鸟类，包括棉鳧、琵嘴鸭、勺嘴鹬等160种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 托马斯·霍斯菲尔德
nodes.summary: 托马斯·霍斯菲尔德是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联23种鸟类，包括栗树鸭、褐翅鸦鹃、Reddish scops owl等23种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: NT
nodes.summary: NT表示 IUCN 近危等级，当前关联914种鸟类，如帝企鹅、小斑几维鸟、大美洲鸵等914种鸟类。

nodes.name: Otus
nodes.summary: Otus（Otus）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联54种鸟类，如White-fronted scops owl、Andaman scops owl、Reddish scops owl等54种鸟类。

nodes.name: 沼泽

nodes.name: 栖息地破坏

nodes.name: 动物界
nodes.summary: 动物界（Animalia）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联10767种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭等10767种鸟类。

nodes.name: Reddish scops owl
nodes.summary: The reddish scops owl (Otus rufescens) is an owl found in southeast Asia, in the countries of Thailand, Malaysia, Indonesia, and Brunei. Description The reddish scops owl measures 15-18 cm in length and has a body mass of 70-83 g. Body is generally a red-brown, with paler face and ear tufts. Displays black spotting across chest, and white spotting along back and wings; juveniles appear similar to adult individuals, but with less spotting. The call of the owl is a whistling “hooo," which is repeated in 7-11 second intervals. The calls of this species are able to be reliably sexed, with males having a higher pitched call than females. Taxonomy The reddish scops owl was first described by Thomas Horsfield in 1821 under the binomial Strix rufescens. The owl has two subspecies: O. r. malayensis O. r. rufescens Habitat The reddish scops owl lives in lowland regions including rainforests and swamps. The owl does not migrate, and primarily inhabits a home range of 1.31–2.16 hectares. Conservation Status The reddish scops owl is evaluated by the IUCN as near threatened, and its population is believed to be decreasing. The primary threat to these birds is habitat loss from logging; an estimated 14%-18% of the forests the owl inhabits have been lost in the last decade. This species is also threatened by capture and sale in illegal wildlife trade. Diet While not extensively studied, the diet of the reddish scops owl consists of primarily arthropods and small vertebrates. Breeding The reddish scops owl nests in tree cavities. On Java, eggs of the species have been found in March and April, with a nestling present by mid-July. References BirdLife Species Factsheet ITIS Report
